steps.title: Spot the parallel -ing verbs
steps.content: The key signal is two actions connected by “and”: “producing more than 350 paintings” and “submitting her research.” Both are participles (verbs with -ing) forming a participial phrase that describes what Beatrix Potter did in her dedication to mycology.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule
steps.content:
A comma precedes a participial phrase that provides additional information about the main clause.

Here, the comma after “fungi” correctly marks “producing... and submitting...” as a participial phrase explaining what her dedication to mycology involved.

The semicolon would wrongly require an independent clause, the period creates a sentence fragment, and no comma leaves the parallel structure unclear.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
